title: tax — subprocessors
description: The Tax module engages up to FOUR third-party subprocessors depending on the tenant's provider config — TaxJar, Avalara AvaTax, Stripe Tax, and (tenant-configured) CustomWebhook. NativeCalculator is INTERNAL (no external processing). Every external provider receives customer address data + purchase amount for tax computation. GDPR Art. 28 DPA references + Art. 30 records of processing activities.

subprocessors.purpose: Server-side tax calculation for US sales tax + international VAT. Handles US Wayfair economic-nexus tracking + EU VAT MOSS + Canadian GST/HST.
data_categories: customer_address (from_country, from_state, from_zip, to_country, to_state, to_zip)
subprocessors.activation_gate: tenant admin acknowledges DPA before saving the provider config

subprocessors.purpose: Enterprise-grade global tax calculation. Handles complex US nexus, per-SKU tax codes, exemption certificate management, VAT MOSS, GST, and 100+ jurisdictions.
data_categories: customer_id + exemption_certificate_id (references to Avalara's certificate system when tenants use ECMS)
subprocessors.activation_gate: tenant admin acknowledges DPA + Avalara's specific tax-professional services agreement

subprocessors.purpose: Automatic tax computation on Stripe-processed transactions. Integrated with Stripe billing — the tenant's Stripe customer + subscription objects carry tax metadata that Stripe Tax computes without a separate calculation call.
subprocessors.activation_gate: tenant admin acknowledges Stripe DPA + confirms Stripe account is provisioned with Stripe Tax feature (Stripe-side upgrade)

subprocessors.purpose: Generic HMAC-signed JSON webhook. Escape hatch for tenants running their own tax computation infrastructure (in-house tax engine, third-party integration not in Academorix's catalog, sovereign-cloud tax service).
data_categories: full calculation request payload — includes customer address + source_amount + source_currency + jurisdiction + applicable exemptions
subprocessors.data_hashing: none by Academorix — caller's endpoint responsible for handling
subprocessors.dpa_url: N/A — the tenant is responsible for their downstream endpoint's DPA if any
subprocessors.activation_gate: tenant admin confirms URL + acknowledges their own DPA responsibility

internal_processors.description: Built-in local computation. Reads tax_rates directly + applies exemptions via ExemptionValidator. NO external processing — the calculation happens inside the Academorix service boundary. Not a subprocessor.
internal_processors.notes: Suitable for single-country tenants + simple jurisdictions. Recommended default for Small-tier tenants.

downstream_subprocessors_referenced.used_for: Dispatching TaxProviderCircuitBreakerOpenedNotification / TaxCalculationDriftDetectedNotification / TaxExemptionVerifiedNotification / TaxExemptionRejectedNotification / TaxExemptionExpiringNotification via mail / SMS / push / Slack channels.

downstream_subprocessors_referenced.reference: compliance::encryption owns the encryption service used for at-rest encryption of tax_provider_configs.config + tax_exemptions.certificate_document_url.

Exemption certificate documents live in a tenant-scoped S3 bucket. AWS S3 is a subprocessor (documented in the compliance/subprocessors global surface). Tax registers the tenant-scoped prefix as a data-processing sub-item.

notes: This file exists deliberately with 4 external subprocessors. Every tenant that configures a non-native provider effectively activates that subprocessor for their tenant. The activation flow prompts the tenant admin to acknowledge the DPA reference before saving.
The tenant-facing subprocessor list (compliance/subprocessors surface) reads from this file + shows only the active subset per tenant (based on which providers the tenant has configured).
NativeCalculator is deliberately NOT listed here — it's internal processing, not a subprocessor engagement. Documenting it as a subprocessor would confuse tenant compliance officers.
Adding a new provider driver = adding a new entry here + updating the tenant-facing subprocessor list + updating compliance.json Art. 28 evidence.
